rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Celestine,

I know this has been a frustrating few weeks, and I really appreciate you sending all those photos and forwarding both order confirmations.

For order R-8801, the mixing bowl set arrived cracked. It was delivered on April 18 and you reported the damage on April 22, which is within the 5-day damage window. I can process a partial refund of $29.40 for the mixing bowl set only. It is not a refund for the full order because the rest of the items in R-8801 — the silicone tray set and the dish rack — arrived undamaged per your own photos, so those are not covered.

For order R-8854, the blender jar came with a cracked lid. The delivery scan shows May 1, and you reported it on May 3. Since that's outside the 5-day window, my options are more limited. What I can do is send one replacement blender lid at no cost as a goodwill gesture, but I cannot process a cash refund for the lid from R-8854.

To move forward, please send me your current shipping address by May 14 so I can queue the replacement lid. I also need you to confirm in this thread that you're okay with the $29.40 partial refund for R-8801. Once I have both confirmations, I'll process the refund and the replacement shipment at the same time. If the $29.40 does not show up within five business days after I process it, please reach back out to me directly.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not change the $59.00 credit amount or the $30.00 post-credit balance.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Bertrand,

I've reviewed your billing dispute, and there are three invoices involved, each with a different situation.

Invoice INV-2210 for $148.00 covers March 1 through March 31, when your team was on the Standard plan. Since you used the service for the full billing period with no mid-month changes, I cannot issue a refund for this invoice.

Invoice INV-2291 for $148.00 covers April 1 through April 30. You downgraded on April 1, but the system charged the Standard rate instead of the $89.00 Basic rate because the plan switch didn't propagate before the invoice ran. This is a billing error, so I can issue a $59.00 credit—the difference between the $148.00 charged and the $89.00 you should have paid. If you confirm by June 5, I'll apply that credit to your next invoice, INV-2361, leaving a balance of $30.00. Invoice INV-2361 for $89.00 covers May 1 through May 31 and is correct.

Please confirm by June 5 so I can process the credit. If I don't hear back by then, the credit window for INV-2291 closes, and the full $89.00 on INV-2361 will remain due.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not include custom dashboard integration in any of the three options.
Do not change the seat price, the API flat fee, or the dedicated onboarding fee.
Do not extend the June 20 expiry date.
Do not offer additional work or scope that is not listed in the three options.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Ingrid,

Thank you for the time on Friday and for the detailed notes your team sent over the weekend. I want to make sure quote Q-5544 is easy to compare against the two add-on options I mentioned, so I am laying everything out in one email.

The base quote Q-5544 is 20 seats at $55 per seat per month, which includes the core platform, onboarding, and standard email support. This quote expires on June 20. The total at this tier is $1,100 per month.

Add-on A is the API access package. If your team adds API access, the seat price stays the same at $55 per seat per month but there is an additional flat fee of $300 per month for API access. That brings the monthly total to $1,400.

Add-on B is the dedicated onboarding program, which is a one-time fee of $1,800 and covers four structured onboarding sessions. The dedicated onboarding is not included in the base quote or Add-on A. If you add both API access and dedicated onboarding, the monthly recurring cost is $1,400 plus the one-time $1,800.

One thing I want to be clear about: the custom dashboard integration your team mentioned on Friday is not in any of these three options. That would require a separate scoping conversation and a separate quote. I cannot include it in Q-5544 or the add-ons without a new approval.

All three options expire on June 20. If you would like to go with one of the options as described, please reply by June 20 and I can send the order form the same day. If you need an extension or want to add the custom dashboard scope, let me know and I can start that process separately.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not say students entering grade 3 in the fall are eligible.
Do not say partial payment is accepted or that a spot can be held without full payment.
Do not extend or soften the June 13 deadline.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hello Ridgemont families,

We’re excited to share details about the Summer Enrichment Program for the 2026 summer session. The program runs July 7 through July 25 on the Ridgemont East campus, Room 104. It is open to students currently enrolled in grades 3 through 6 at Ridgemont. Students entering grade 3 in the fall are not eligible for this session.

Enrollment is capped at 24 students this session. If more than 24 completed applications come in, we’ll start a waitlist in the order we receive them. Being on the interest list from last year does not guarantee a spot — all families must submit a new application this year. The program fee is $185 per student, due with the application. Applications and payment must reach the main office by Friday, June 13 at 4 p.m. The application form is Form SEP-2026, available at the main office window or on the school website. We cannot accept partial payment, and we cannot hold a spot without payment.

If a student ends up on the waitlist, we’ll hold the $185 payment until a spot opens or the deadline passes — if no spot opens, we’ll return the payment. Questions? Email enrichment@example.com or stop by the main office Tuesday through Thursday, 8 a.m. to 3 p.m.

Best,

Ceramic pour-over carafe (SKU CPV-11): The return label was emailed on June 3, but we haven't received the package yet. Once it arrives, I can process a $34.00 refund to your card within 3–5 business days.

Stainless travel cup (SKU STC-07): The package arrived on June 5 and we confirmed it's undamaged and unused. The $22.50 refund was issued on June 6 and should appear on your statement within 2 business days.

Replacement lid (SKU LID-04): You noted the lid was cracked on arrival. I need one photo of the cracked lid before I can send a replacement. Please send the photo by June 10 at noon, or the replacement request will close automatically.

I can't issue a refund for the pour-over carafe until we physically receive the item, and I can't send the replacement lid before the photo arrives. The rest is moving along.

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not interpret or describe the contents of the imaging records.
Do not say the release has already been processed or is in progress.
Do not accept a paper form photo as an alternative to the portal submission.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Gwendolyn,

I'm following up on your request to send your imaging records from your April 18 appointment to Dr. Okafor's office. We can't release records without your signed authorization, so here's what to do.

Log in to the patient portal at portal.example.org, go to "My Records" and then "Release Authorization." Complete the Release of Medical Records form, selecting Dr. Okafor's practice as the recipient and checking "Diagnostic Imaging" under record type. Sign and submit the form inside the portal—please don't email a photo of a paper form, as only the portal version is accepted.

Once we have the signed form, our records team typically processes release requests within 5 business days. If you haven't heard anything after 7 business days, call the records office at ext. 3114. I can't give you a status or contact Dr. Okafor's office until the authorization is in our system. If this is urgent, please ask Dr. Okafor's office to mark the incoming request as urgent and fax a priority cover sheet to 555-0192.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not diagnose or label Soren behaviorally.
Do not say the check-in with Ms. Vega is disciplinary.
Do not blame Ingrid or suggest parenting issues.
rows.RewrittenText:
Dear Ingrid,

I’m writing to share an observation from May 15 so we can work together on the right support for Soren. During independent reading time that day, Soren pushed another student’s book off the desk. When I asked him to return to his seat, he said he didn’t care and turned away. I want to be clear that I’m not labeling Soren or suggesting there’s something wrong with him—I’m simply sharing what I saw.

Since May 15, I’ve checked in with Soren each morning at arrival, and he’s been calm and engaged in those moments. I’ve also added his name to Ms. Vega’s list for a voluntary check-in. Ms. Vega is our school counselor who works with students in these situations, and this is not a disciplinary step.

I’d like to meet with you to talk through this properly. I’m available on May 22 after 3:30 p.m. or May 24 between 9 and 10 a.m. Please let me know which works for you, and we can confirm by end of day May 21.

Best,

rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Octavia,

Thank you for the call on May 16 and for sharing the detailed feature request summary for Kessler Group. I want to give you an honest response to avoid misaligned expectations.

The two features your team outlined — bulk role assignment via CSV and a dedicated audit log export API — are on the product roadmap, but they are not committed to a specific release date. I cannot tell you they will be ready by your contract renewal on September 1 because our product team has not confirmed a timeline yet. If either feature lands before renewal, I will notify you immediately.

Kessler Group's contract, account ID KG-00412, runs through August 31, and the renewal at the current rate is $3,400 per month for the 40-seat Enterprise plan. On May 17, I submitted a formal feature priority request on your behalf, reference FPR-2241, so your use case is documented in the product backlog. I can also connect you with our enterprise support team for a more detailed roadmap conversation.

Please reply if you would like that introduction, and I will set it up by the end of this week. I just cannot make a commitment that custom development or a discounted renewal will happen to close this gap.

Best,
